title: drosera collina
intro: Drosera collina, also known as the Australian sundew or low-lying sundew, is a carnivorous plant species native to Australia. It belongs to the Droseraceae family and is characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest small insects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Drosera collina are often used to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to soothe digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
